Patch Set 2:

Initial testing failed:

> runtests@ldiskfs+DNE Client crashed(125s)
- (Untriaged #69020, seen 1 times before)
> runtests-ssk@ldiskfs+SharedKey Client crashed(682s)
- (Untriaged #69020, seen 2 times before)
> runtests@zfs Client crashed(105s)
- (Untriaged #69020, seen 0 times before)

(centos7)All results and logs: https://testing.whamcloud.com/gerrit-janitor/33264/results.html

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

I don't think we want to do this.
This isn't really a refcount in that nothing gets freed or released when the count reaches zero, and it isn't at all clear that the count cannot increase again after getting down to zero.

I think this should remain as an atomic_t.  It is simply a count of pending IO requests.

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/96683/ : SUCCESS

commit.subject: LU-16796 osc: Change struct osc_object to use kref
commit.message:
LU-16796 osc: Change struct osc_object to use kref

This patch changes struct osc_object to use
kref(refcount_t) instead of atomic_t

Signed-off-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Change-Id: I55490f645f7bfccc8b66aa47ba2ae1da61feb795
